title: i386 (only) test failure: Error opening grom.fst as FST file
body:
Hi,

I'm seeing the `test/sat/grom.ys` test fail on Debian i386 (but not on amd64 or any other architecture). I tried to bisect the issue in the YosysHQ/yosys repo, but even as far back as yosys-0.23 I'm seeing the test fail now altough it must have passed just fine as part of the 0.23 Debian package builds see buildd history below. 

Repro, in a Debian i386 chroot:

```
$ echo CONFIG:=gcc > Makefile.conf
$ make -j32
$ ( cd tests/sat/; ../../yosys grom.ys )
[...]
7. Executing SIM pass (simulate the circuit).
ERROR: Error opening 'grom.fst' as FST file
```

Full grom.log in yosys repo on top of 6405bbab1e8afc587febf41339aaf8b514c8f202 (Fri Aug 18 00:14:07 2023 +0000): https://gist.github.com/DanielG/084cf786c57afc4050dd5f874bee7869
Debian i386 buildd log: https://buildd.debian.org/status/fetch.php?pkg=yosys&arch=i386&ver=0.30-2&stamp=1692708361&raw=0
Debian i386 buildd history, showing that 0.23 should work: https://buildd.debian.org/status/logs.php?pkg=yosys&arch=i386 

A quick debug session shows this break being triggered (in libs/fst/fstapi.cc):
```
                        if (vu.d != FST_DOUBLE_ENDTEST) {
                            break; /* either corrupt file or wrong architecture (offset +33 also functions as matchword)
                                    */
                        }
```

but I haven't had time to dig further into it yet.

--Daniel
code_blocks:
$ echo CONFIG:=gcc > Makefile.conf
$ make -j32
$ ( cd tests/sat/; ../../yosys grom.ys )
[...]
